items.title: PDRM seizes nearly 500,000 illicit files in sweeping child exploitation crackdown across Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) announced today the seizure of 498,694 digital files in a single sweeping crackdown on online child sexual exploitation in Malaysia, carried out as part of a recent seven-country operation targeting cross-border networks involved in the sharing of child sexual abuse material (CSAM). Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said 69 out of 101 identified CSAM enablers were tracked down and arrested in Malaysia following extensive intelligence-sharing with regional and international law enforcement partners. With the main operation taking place from March 23 to April 17, Mohd Khalid said PDRM alongside its state contingents conducted simultaneous arrests nationwide between April 6 and April 8.

items.title: Zahid: Anwar's ties could help free Malaysians held after flotilla interception
items.summary: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's strong international relations could help facilitate negotiations. "God willing, I will ask the Foreign Minister to make efforts and play his role," he told reporters. Zahid said Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan should play an active role in assisting the detained Malaysian delegates.

items.title: Transport Ministry extends Madani30 30pc ETS, ERT discount to May 14 after strong response
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The 30 per cent discount offered under the Madani30 promotional code for Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) Electric Train Service (ETS) and Ekspres Rakyat Timuran (ERT) services has been extended until May 14. The Ministry of Transport (MOT), in a statement today, said the extension was made following encouraging public response, reflecting the public’s readiness to shift towards more affordable and convenient rail transport. “In this regard, MOT urges the public to take advantage of this opportunity by planning their trips in advance and using KTMB services to their preferred destinations,” it said.

items.title: 11 squatter areas in Sabah's Kapayan flagged as fire-prone
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Eleven squatter settlements at high risk of fire have been identified in Kapayan, said the Assistant Minister of Local Government and Housing. Based on records from the Penampang Municipal Council (MPP), Datuk Maijol Mahap said the settlements are located on river reserves, along drains and on hilly terrain. He was responding to a question from (Warisan–Kapayan) Chin Tek Ming during the state legislative assembly on the number of squatter settlements at high risk of fire in the area.

items.title: Michael Chong calls for revival of preventive laws to combat loan shark abuses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MCA Public Services and Complaints Department head Datuk Seri Michael Chong has proposed reinstating relevant preventive laws to more effectively combat increasingly rampant illegal lending operations. He said the proposal was made during a courtesy visit to the Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) by the MCA Public Services and Complaints Department and the Malaysia–China Friendship and Mutual Assistance Association today. Chong also submitted numerous complaint cases received by the department to Rusdi.

items.title: Ekuinas elevates art economy push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ekuiti Nasional Bhd's (Ekuinas) Iltizam Seni exhibition returned this year with a sharper ambition to position art not merely as cultural expression but as a catalyst for economic value, entrepreneurship and long-term industry growth in Malaysia. Now in its third cycle, Iltizam Seni, the arts pillar under Ekuinas' broader social impact platform Iltizam, was conceived to create a more deliberate pathway for emerging Bumiputera artists to move beyond passion and into sustainable, income-generating careers. But art is also industry.

items.title: Zahid says BN lawmakers' move aimed at upholding royal institution, adat
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the move by all 14 Barisan Nasional assemblymen in Negri Sembilan to withdraw support for the Menteri Besar was made in good faith to ensure the state's royal institution and adat customs are respected and upheld by the people. He said the action should not be misconstrued as an attempt to topple the state government or form a new administration through the back door. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Foreign property buyers turn to Malaysia amid geopolitical tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ongoing geopolitical tensions are reshaping investor flows into Malaysia's property market with GD Properties seeing increased interest from foreign buyers, particularly from the Middle East. GD Properties group chief executive Ng Khoon Hour said uncertainties in parts of the world have prompted investors to look toward more stable markets in Asia. "The tensions around the world have built the expectation that Malaysia is a safe market," he said.

items.title: Witness tells court former Perlis MB Azlan Man received RM1.06m from cancelled state-funded travel packages
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The Sessions Court was told today that there were elements of misappropriation of Perlis state government property when Datuk Seri Azlan Man received funds resulting from the cancellation of overseas travel packages for trips that never took place. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Senior Superintendent M. Theenmoly, 41, testified that investigations revealed Azlan — through his then-Senior Private Secretary, Herwardy Hashim — had instructed the agencies Sri Kedawang Travel & Tour (W) Sdn Bhd (SKTT) and Aidil Travel & Tours Sdn Bhd to cancel the travel bookings. Therefore, I found evidence showing that Datuk Seri Azlan Man received money from illegal activities, namely the cancellation of overseas vacation packages,” she said.

items.title: All 14 BN assemblymen to continue backing Negri Sembilan unity govt
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: All 14 Barisan Nasional assemblymen in Negri Sembilan have agreed to continue supporting the unity government in the state following a meeting with Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today. Zahid said the decision was reached after discussions with the assemblymen, amid controversy surrounding their earlier withdrawal of support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. "My meeting with all the assemblymen showed that while we respect the decision made by the assemblymen concerned, the party at the national level and all the assemblymen I met today agreed that we should continue together with the unity government in Negri Sembilan so that there will be no disruption.

items.title: Probe into commando's critical injuries at camp completed, say police
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have completed their investigation into the case of a commando who suffered severe injuries at a military camp in Johor in March. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the investigation paper was submitted to the state prosecution director on Tuesday. Hamid, who is attached to the 22nd Commando Regiment at Kem Iskandar in Mersing, reportedly sustained serious injuries on March 11 after a fall.

items.title: What happened at Johor military camp? Family questions how healthy soldier ended up in vegetative state
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 30 — The family of a Grup Gerak Khas (GGK) personnel from the 22 Commando Regiment, Trooper Abdul Hamid Talib, has urged the government to provide an explanation and conduct a thorough investigation into the severe injuries he sustained while at camp. Lawyers for Liberty (LFL) adviser N. Surendran, who is also the family’s spokesperson, said at a press conference today that the family had yet to receive a reasonable explanation as to how the 25-year-old victim suffered such serious injuries, leaving him in a vegetative state. Petaling Jaya District Police chief Shamsudin Mamat, when contacted, confirmed that family members had lodged a second police report at the Petaling Jaya district police headquarters today.

items.title: Huawei boosts Malaysia's smart energy push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Huawei Digital Power has launched its FusionSolar 9.0 Smart photovoltaic (PV) and energy storage system (ESS) solution in Malaysia. This marks a major shift from conventional solar systems to intelligent, grid-stabilising and artificial intelligence (AI)-driven energy infrastructure, boosting Malaysia's transition toward more intelligent, resilient and sustainable energy systems. Huawei Malaysia executive vice president of digital power business Chong Chern Peng said Malaysia is making steady progress in its clean energy transition.

items.title: Criminals, traffic violators beware: DBKL’s 10,000 CCTVs can now identify faces, track your routes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The Kuala Lumpur City Hall today said all of its 10,000 CCTV cameras have been upgraded with high resolution facial recognition technology that could help authorities monitor, identify law breakers and respond swiftly. The upgrade, announced by Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and DBKL Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud here, is part of a large-scale roll out of an integrated system that would connect monitoring, enforcement and city management capabilities. “If you think about doing crime in KL, don’t.” Also present was Kuala Lumpur Police Chief, CP Datuk Fadil Marsus.

items.title: Mamdani calls on King Charles to return Koh-i-Noor diamond
items.summary: NEW YORK: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani called for King Charles to "return" the prized Koh-i-Noor diamond, which the British Empire took from the Indian subcontinent in the 1800s, on the third day of the monarch's state visit on Wednesday. Before greeting Charles and Queen Camilla at a 9/11 memorial event, Mamdani was asked what he would discuss with the king if he had the chance. Housed in the Tower of London, the massive 106 carat stone is the star of Britain's crown jewels, adorning the Crown of Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother.

items.title: Search for 10-year-old boy who fell in Puncak Alam drain ends after three days; body found in Sungai Buloh
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 30 — The body of a 10-year-old boy, who went missing after reportedly falling into a drain in Bandar Puncak Alam on Tuesday (28), was found in Sungai Buloh today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mokhtar said the body was found at about 1 pm, about six kilometres (km) from where he was reported to have fallen at Lorong Cakera Purnama 12/27. “This integrated operation also involved the JBPM’s Water Rescue Unit (PPDA) and K9 Tracker Dog Unit,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Thousands of bikers expected at RXZ Fighter 3.0 gathering in Kuantan
items.summary: KUANTAN: Biking enthusiasts are set to converge on the state capital for the RXZ Fighter 3.0 gathering, which will be held at Serambi Teruntum in Tanjung Lumpur on May 1. Pahang Communications and Multimedia, Youth, Sports and Non-Governmental Organisations Committee chairman Fadzli Mohamad Kamal said Kuantan will once again play host to the two-day RXZ gathering, which begins tomorrow. "I welcome RXZ fans from all over the country to Kuantan in conjunction with RXZ Fighter 3.0.

items.title: RM230mil misappropriation case: NGO rep, company director on bail
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A deputy chairman of a non-governmental organisation and a company director have been released on bail following an investigation into the alleged misappropriation of RM230 million in NGO funds. Selangor Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) director Datuk Mohd Azwan Ramli confirmed the release, adding that all suspects in the case are now out on MACC bail. The case emerged earlier this month following MACC raids on several premises in Selangor and Kuala Lumpur, including offices linked to the NGO and the company.

items.title: Man heartbroken after failing to save disabled neighbour in fire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 26-year-old man risked his life to rescue two elderly neighbours from a fire in Taman Ehsan early today, but was heartbroken after failing to save their disabled son. He came to the front of my house and shouted about the fire. "I thought my own house was on fire, but I ran out and saw the house behind me burning.

items.title: Jawi opposes Rain Rave Water Music Festival over moral concerns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (Jawi) has opposed the proposed Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026, saying it could undermine moral values and social harmony. In a statement today, Jawi expressed regret over the proposed programme and urged organisers to review its concept, stressing the need to respect Malaysia's multi-racial, multi-religious and cultural sensitivities. It cited Bayan Linnas Series 351, a series of public religious rulings, guidance, and explanations, issued by the Federal Territories Mufti's Department, which states that entertainment is permissible in Islam but must comply with Syariah guidelines.

items.title: IGP urges restraint over N. Sembilan political crisis, assures public order intact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has urged for calm and restraint amid an ongoing political crisis in Negeri Sembilan, as law enforcement work to ensure public order. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said what is currently taking place in Negeri Sembilan relates to customs practiced and the public should allow due process to proceed without interference. Mohd Khalid said numerous police reports have been lodged by relevant parties, mostly related to dissatisfaction, but did not provide their breakdown.

items.title: [UPDATED] IGP urges calm amid Negri Sembilan Adat Perpatih crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail has urged all quarters to remain calm and refrain from making unnecessary statements over the crisis involving Negri Sembilan's Adat Perpatih customary system and its constitutional framework. On April 27, 14 Negri Sembilan Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen withdrew their support for the state government led by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, citing a crisis involving the state's Adat Perpatih customary system and its constitutional framework. Negri Sembilan Umno Liaison Committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias had said the party leadership had taken note of developments involving the state's Adat Perpatih system and its interaction with the state constitutional framework.

items.title: Kelantan man with schizophrenia facing murder charge for killing 83-year-old dad sent for mental assessment
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 30 — A mentally disabled man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with murdering his father last week. Deputy Public Prosecutor Mohd Nadzir Abdullah said the post-mortem report had not yet been received and asked for the man to be sent to undergo a psychiatric assessment. Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid ordered the accused, who is represented by lawyers Tengku Shazuan Tengku Shariffudin and Ahmad Nizam Mohamed, to be sent to Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, Perak, for a psychiatric assessment under Section 342 of the Criminal Procedure Code.

items.title: Viral clip of ‘emaciated’ Malayan tiger in Japan sparks renewed concerns over welfare of Malaysian animals abroad (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — A viral social media post showing a visibly underweight Malayan tiger in a Japanese zoo has stirred concern among Malaysians, intensifying debate over the welfare of Malaysian wildlife sent overseas. The discussion has since expanded to the welfare of three Malaysian elephants — Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK) — currently housed at Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan. According to a report published in The Star yesterday, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup has said the elephants should be brought back to Malaysia and relocated to the Taiping Zoo and Night Safari.

items.title: One life lost, three saved: Terengganu man’s organs give new hope to 13-year-old girl, 19-year-old man, and 47-year-old woman
items.summary: KUALA BERANG, April 30 — The widow of organ donor the late Muhammad Syukri Bazli is grateful that her husband’s sacrifice helped save three lives, including two teenagers. Nurul Hanis Amelia Ramli said based on information from medical officers at Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital (HSNZ), her late husband’s liver was donated to a 13-year-old girl, while his kidneys were given to a 19-year-old male teenager and a 47-year-old woman. The mother of three children aged between one and six said that after news of the organ donation spread, many visitors came to offer support and contributions, which helped ease her family’s burden.

items.title: Mubarak ordered to vacate Sungei Ujong Undang official residence after 30 years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — Datuk Mubarak Dohak has been ordered to immediately vacate the official residence at Balai Luak Undang Sungei Ujong, where he has stayed for more than three decades. “In view of this, you are instructed to vacate the official residence and Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong with immediate effect. The dispute over the Sungei Ujong adat institution surfaced after Waris Luak Sungei Ujong issued a statement on March 18 announcing Mubarak’s removal as Datuk Klana Petra Undang Luak Sungei Ujong, alleging breaches of religious and customary law.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police nab 69 in nationwide crackdown on online child sexual exploitation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have detained 69 people, including civil servants, in a nationwide crackdown on online child sexual exploitation under Operation Cyber Guardian 2026. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the operation was part of a multi-national effort involving seven countries: Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Brunei, Hong Kong, South Korea and Japan. "Preliminary checks also uncovered 498,694 digital files, comprising 204,934 child sexual abuse material (CSAM) files and 293,760 adult pornographic files," he said.

items.title: Work-from-home civil servants help cut petrol subsidy bill by RM1.9m since April 15
items.summary: KLUANG, April 30 — The implementation of the work-from-home (WFH) initiative has begun to show a significant impact, saving RM1.9 million in RON95 petrol subsidies, equivalent to 979,632 litres, over 10 days since April 15. Director-General of Public Service (KPPA) Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz said the savings involved 120,816 litres of petrol, or RM239,215.68 per day. He said 20,136 civil servants are currently participating in the WFH arrangement, up from 15,452 on April 15.

items.title: Survivor of SMK Tanjong Mas crash that killed classmate leaves hospital after two weeks, but still needs more surgeries
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 30 — A Form One student, who was seriously injured after being rammed by an SUV in an accident at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Tanjong Mas on April 12, was discharged from hospital on Saturday. Kelantan Health director Datuk Dr Mohd Azman Yacob said the 13-year-old girl underwent a skin graft procedure on her earlobe last week. “Several follow-up treatments are still required, with an estimated four to five more procedures as this involves surgery,” he told reporters at Raja Perempuan Zainab II Hospital here today.

items.title: China challenges Western model, pitches its path to modernisation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: China is ready to share its experience of modernisation with developing countries and support them in charting their own paths based on national conditions, China's Ambassador to Malaysia Ouyang Yujing said. Ouyang said China's development path could serve as a reference for other developing nations. "This year marks the first year of China's 15th Five-Year Plan and Malaysia's 13th Malaysia Plan.

items.title: Teenager fined RM3,000 for injuring traffic policeman at roadblock
items.summary: Magistrate Siti Zubaidah Mahat handed down the sentence to Muhammad Irfan Jasman, 19, after he pleaded guilty to the charge. The court also ordered the accused to serve three months in jail if he fails to pay the fine. The charge under Section 332 of the Penal Code carries a maximum imprisonment of three years, a fine, or both, upon conviction.

items.title: Foreigner pleads guilty to possessing obscene material on phone
items.summary: PONTIAN: A foreigner pleaded guilty at the Pontian magistrate's court today to charges of possessing obscene material and nude videos on his mobile phone on April 26. The accused, Santosh Mukhiya, 24, a Nepali, said he understood the charges read out before magistrate Nurhafiza Haron. The accused is also the complainant's former boyfriend.

items.title: New cross‑border taxi rules kick in May 4 as Singapore and Malaysia widen routes and options
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — Cross border taxi services between Singapore and Malaysia will be expanded from May 4 to give travellers more flexibility while ensuring fair competition among operators, the transport ministries of both countries announced today. The enhancements, agreed following the 12th Singapore Malaysia Leaders’ Retreat in December 2025, will allow licensed taxis to drop passengers anywhere in Singapore and in Johor Bahru, Iskandar Puteri, Forest City, Kulai and Senai. Vacant carriage entry will generally be prohibited, except during specified exemption periods — Fridays for Malaysia registered taxis entering Singapore, and Sundays for Singapore registered taxis entering Malaysia.

items.title: Immigration extends migrant repatriation programme to May 2027
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has extended the Migrant Repatriation Programme 2.0 (PRM 2.0) from May 1 to May 31, 2027, after it was due to end today. In a statement, it said the decision followed a positive assessment of the programme, citing strong registrations, steady voluntary returns and encouraging compound collections. Between May 19, 2025 and April 29, 2026, the programme recorded 254,186 registrations involving migrants from 112 countries, with total collections amounting to RM127 million.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines to resume Doha flights on July 2
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines Bhd will resume limited services to Doha from July 2, following Qatar's reopening of its airspace to international carriers and the gradual reinstatement of operations at Hamad International Airport (HIA). Malaysia Airlines said the reopening of operations follows a "notice to airmen" (Notam) by Qatar Civil Aviation Authority confirming operational readiness. "Malaysia Airlines operations will initially be limited to one daily service (MH160/161).

items.title: Tourism ministry to comply with spending cuts, keep Visit Malaysia 2026 plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry (Motac) said it will comply with Treasury expenditure cuts while maintaining key initiatives, including Visit Malaysia 2026, as planned. The ministry's secretary-general Datuk Shaharuddin Abu Sohot said Motac would fully comply with the government's directive on expenditure controls, as outlined by the Finance Ministry, in line with efforts to ensure prudent fiscal management. "Motac emphasises that the approach aligns with the government's priority of maintaining responsible fiscal governance, with national interest remaining the core consideration in all spending decisions," he said.

items.title: Malaysian Bar calls for removal of gender equality reservations, warns of possible constitutional challenge
items.summary: Full compliance with Article 8(2) of the Federal Constitution is necessary to ensure meaningful gender equality, Malaysian Bar President Anand Raj said in a statement. “These reservations are inconsistent with the constitutional guarantee of gender equality enshrined in Article 8(2) of the FC, which expressly prohibits discrimination on the grounds of gender. He said Malaysia’s continued retention of certain reservations under CEDAW undermines efforts to achieve full gender equality despite earlier reforms and advocacy efforts by civil society groups and institutions.

items.title: Sisters' drug use uncovered in Klang raid
items.summary: "Eventually, we became addicted and sometimes took the drugs together," she said after being detained during Op Perdana with her boyfriend. The sister said Nada had previously detained them. "Initially, I did not know my sister was taking drugs.

items.title: Bersatu info chief apologises after dog sticker post sparks outrage, says conduct was inappropriate
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 30 — Melaka Bersatu information chief Datuk Hishamuddin Abdul Karim has apologised to state executive councillor Datuk Abdul Razak Abdul Rahman after a party assemblyman posted an image of him online with a dog sticker covering his face. According to Free Malaysia Today (FMT), Hishamuddin said he viewed the Facebook post by Sungai Udang representative Dr Mohd Aleef Yusof as inappropriate and disparaging. Earlier, Masjid Tanah MP Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin criticised Mohd Aleef over the Facebook post, saying it was inappropriate for both a Muslim and an elected representative.

items.title: Father jailed for raping teenage daughter while she slept
items.summary: MUAR: A 37-year-old man was sentenced to 20 years' imprisonment and 13 strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to raping and sexually assaulting his 15-year-old daughter. Judge Mohd Khairi Haron handed down the sentence after considering the facts of the case, the accused's admission of guilt and submissions by the prosecution. According to the charges, the accused, a grass cutter, committed sexual assault on the victim while she was asleep in her bedroom at their home in Tangkak between 8pm and 9pm last year.

items.title: Letter ordering vacation of Undang Luak Sungei Ujong's residence circulates
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A letter purportedly from the Negri Sembilan State Secretary's Office is circulating, directing the immediate vacation of the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong official residence and the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong. The April 29 letter is addressed to the private secretary of Datuk Mubarak Dohak, who was stripped of his position as the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong earlier this month. "Accordingly, you are directed to vacate the official residence and Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong with immediate effect.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia Q1 net profit up 6.4pct to RM72.8mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's net profit rose 6.4 per cent to RM72.83 million in the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026 from RM68.42 million a year ago. This was supported by robust core-market activity and sustained momentum in initiatives to strengthen participation and market vibrancy. In a statement, Bursa Malaysia said its operating revenue grew 16.4 per cent year on year (YoY) to RM206.9 million.

items.title: Khaled: Defence cuts will not affect readiness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said planned operating expenditure cuts will not significantly affect the country's defence readiness and capability. Yesterday, the Finance Ministry proposed a RM5.4 billion reduction in operating expenditure across government sectors as part of efforts to strengthen the country's fiscal position. Of the total, RM14.11 billion is allocated for operating expenditure, while RM7.63 billion is for development expenditure.

items.title: Army major claims trial to RM225,000 false claims charges
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: An army major claimed trial at the Ayer Kerod Sessions Court here today to five charges of submitting false claims involving maintenance work and non-existent raw material supplies worth over RM225,000 two years ago. According to the charge sheet, the accused, while serving as the Commanding Officer of the 9th Squadron of the Royal Engineers Regiment (9 SKN RAJD) at Terendak Camp, allegedly used five invoices from different companies containing false details related to construction and maintenance work. All the offences were allegedly committed at the 9 SKN RAJD Office at Terendak Camp between March 12 and Nov 19, 2024.

items.title: No splurges, no risks: Ipsos says Malaysians tighten wallets as inflation bites
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — Consumer confidence among Malaysians declined steeply in April as inflation continued to soar, but remained considerably higher than that of their international peers, an Ipsos report said today. The Ipsos Consumer Confidence Index for April 2026 noted that confidence in Malaysia dropped by 6.1 per cent, the second‑sharpest decline after Thailand in the region. This is despite inflation in Malaysia recording an eight per cent month‑on‑month increase in April.

items.title: Commando critically injured after alleged punishment, LFL urges attempted murder classification
items.summary: LFL lawyer N Surendran said the family and legal team have yet to receive any satisfactory explanation regarding the incident involving Truper Abdul Hamid Talib, 25. He said the victim is currently in critical condition, unconscious, unable to speak, and reliant on medical assistance for breathing and nutrition via a feeding tube. Surendran said information received indicates that the injuries occurred while punishment was being carried out at the camp on March 11.

items.title: Sabah bans open burning as El Niño raises forest fire risk
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 30 — The Sabah state government has issued a circular banning open burning, including land clearing for agriculture, during the hot weather period linked to El Niño to reduce the risk of forest and plantation fires. Sabah Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said the move is a precautionary step ahead of the hot, dry weather expected between April and August this year. “Hot weather could trigger small fires from open burning or land clearing.

items.title: Tunku Ismail praises Johor’s RM272b investment haul, says record RM110b in 2025 marks history
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, April 30 — Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail praised the state’s economic achievements, which have recorded impressive figures over the past four years with investments totalling RM272 billion. He said the achievement also includes Johor’s success in creating history with investments worth RM110 billion last year. Tunku Ismail said this figure was the highest ever recorded by any state in Malaysia.

items.title: Elderly man suffers severe burns in Kuantan workshop fire
items.summary: KUANTAN: An elderly man suffered severe burns after a lorry workshop was partially destroyed in a fire at Lorong Bukit Setongkol here today. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said the Kuantan fire station was alerted to the incident at 8.45am. He sustained burns across his body," he said in a statement.

items.title: New Malaysia–Indonesia pact to tap health, education and cultural travel
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — Malaysia and Indonesia have agreed to strengthen cooperation in the tourism industry by focusing on the development of multi-segment products, including cultural experiences, educational exchanges, as well as health and wellness tourism. Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said the agreement was reached during a dinner with representatives of Indonesia’s key tourism industry associations as part of his recent working visit to the country. “All parties at the meeting agreed that tourism product content must continue to be enriched, encompassing cultural experiences, educational exchanges as well as health and wellness tourism to meet the needs of various visitor segments,” he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Mas Ermieyati slams Bersatu rep’s dog sticker post, says it insults Muslim manners and elected office
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 30 — Masjid Tanah MP Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin has criticised Sungai Udang assemblyman Dr Mohd Aleef Yusoh over a Facebook post showing a Melaka exco member’s face covered with a cartoon dog sticker. Mohd Aleef had earlier uploaded a photo believed to show Melaka tourism, heritage, arts and culture committee chairman Datuk Abdul Razak Abdul Rahman, with the sticker placed over his head. Mohd Aleef said that his Facebook post was in response to Abdul Razak’s TikTok video about him after the assembly session.

items.title: Kedah gets first Cold Storage outlet in Alor Setar as RM350m Tenz Emporium takes shape
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 30 — Premium supermarket Cold Storage’s first outlet in Kedah, at The Tenz Emporium here, is expected to boost the local economy and lifestyle landscape of the city. GCH Retail (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd operates Cold Storage, including operating over 90 stores under brands such as Giant, Mercato, Cold Storage, and TMC. In October last year, Imperio Group signed a lease agreement with GCH Retail (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd to open the Cold Storage outlet at Tenz Emporium in Alor Setar.

items.title: Ten Malaysians missing after Israeli forces board Gaza aid flotilla, says Sumud Nusantara Command Center
items.summary: BANGI, April 30 — The Sumud Nusantara Command Center (SNCC) has confirmed that 22 vessels under the Global Sumud Flotilla 2.0 (GSF2.0) mission were intercepted and boarded by Israeli forces in international waters en route to Gaza, including six with 10 Malaysians on board. SNCC director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said they are Zainal Rashid Ahmad on board Arkham III; Osman Zolkifli and Dr Jihan Alya Mohd Nordin (Eros 1); Hazwan Hazim Dermawan, Mohd Hanafi Mohd Salim, Ahmad Musa Al-Nuwayri Kamaruzaman and Norhelmi Ab Ghani (Bianca BCN). Sani Araby, who is also chief executive of the Malaysian Consultative Council of Islamic Organisations, said the GSF2.0 mission involved 63 vessels in total, but only 22 were intercepted, lost contact and directly boarded, affecting a total of 186 activists.

items.title: Malaysia Airlines returns to Doha from July 2, only one daily flight for now
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — Malaysia Airlines will resume limited services to Doha (DOH) from July 2, 2026, following Qatar’s reopening of its airspace to international carriers and the gradual reinstatement of operations at Hamad International Airport (HIA), according to Malaysia Aviation Group Bhd (MAG). “Malaysia Airlines operations will initially be limited to one daily service (MH160/161). MAG said the airline continues to closely monitor the situation through ongoing risk assessments and relevant NOTAMs before fully resuming operations.

items.title: Anthony Loke says Zahid rejects backdoor bid, insists Negeri Sembilan Unity Government remains intact and stable
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — DAP secretary‑general Anthony Loke has said Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi does not support the formation of a new government or a “backdoor administration” in Negeri Sembilan. As reported by Berita Harian (BH), Loke, who is also Transport Minister, said Zahid conveyed the position in his capacity as deputy prime minister during yesterday’s Cabinet meeting, following the move by 14 Umno assemblymen to withdraw support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. “This means the existing government remains the Unity Government in Negeri Sembilan, ensuring stability and continuity in the state’s administration.

items.title: Penang targets 600 EV charging bays by 2030 as green push gathers pace
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 30 — Penang aims to install 600 electric vehicle (EV) charging bays in the state by 2030, said Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. He said there are currently a few hundred, between 200 and 300 EV charging bays already installed in the state. He said even public buildings and shopping centres are converting some parking bays into EV charging bays.

items.title: No licence, no blood tests, Health Ministry tells community pharmacies
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 30 — The Health Ministry (MOH) has stressed that health screening services, including blood collection offered by community pharmacies, may only be carried out at registered and licensed premises in line with existing laws. It said under Section 2 of the Private Healthcare Facilities and Services Act 1998 [Act 586], any premises offering health screening services, including blood collection, must be registered and licensed under the Act. “Therefore, under the current legal framework, any premises carrying out blood collection is considered to be providing healthcare services and must be registered and licensed under Act 586.

items.title: Johor Regent urges federal govt to expedite rail link to RTS to ease congestion
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, April 30 — Johor Regent, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, today called on the federal government to expedite approval for a rail transit system that will integrate with the upcoming Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link. “There is an urgent need to ensure that Johor is prepared to face the increased movement of people. The RTS Link, scheduled to begin operations on January 1, 2027, is a four-kilometre (km) shuttle service connecting Bukit Chagar in Johor Bahru to Woodlands in Singapore.

items.title: Accident-prone roads first as Sabah gets RM17.9m for street light fixes
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 30 — The allocation for the maintenance and repair of street lights along federal roads in Sabah has been increased to RM17.9 million this year, from RM8 million annually in 2024 and 2025, said State Assistant Minister of Works and Utilities Datuk Limus Jury. He said the increased allocation reflected the government’s continued commitment to addressing the issue of faulty street lights in the state. “Street lights on federal roads are federal government assets and their maintenance is fully funded through federal allocations,” he said in reply to a question from Justin Wong Yung Bin (Warisan-Sri Tanjong) on the allocation for street light maintenance on federal roads in Sabah at the State Legislative Assembly sitting here today.

items.title: Housewife in Sepang pleads not guilty to posting false fuel price claim on Threads
items.summary: SEPANG, April 30 — A housewife pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of making false content relating to fuel price increases in the country via social media. Siti Nor Sayikilla Mohd Azhar, 31, is accused of knowingly creating and initiating the transmission of false communications on March 31 with intent to annoy others, using @cek_apple95 account on Threads. Deputy public prosecutor Mohamad Azmir Mohd Razali appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was represented by lawyer Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali.
